Now I am a person who typically buys premium when I can, but I am noticing that the prices keep slowly increasing a NOTICEABLE amount (not that the price was ever reasonable). Overall if you don’t have the money to spend, STAY AWAY FROM SPOTIFY; and even if you do—it’s still not that great of an app, you should spend it elsewhere

Favorite app ever. I personally love this app. I have had it for over 4 years, and it’s been an app I use daily. One thing I will say is frustrating is the constant change of the like button to songs, they removed the heart for whatever reason and with that they made adding a song to a playlist but not your liked songs a lot harder, with a drop down feature you have to scroll down to find the playlist you want. I will say that I love that you can get to choose if you want your playlists public or not because let's be honest some playlists do not need to see the light of day but can still be enjoyed in peace. I also like what they did with the front page, being able to just swipe right to click onto our profile and access our settings much easier. My most favorite feature that might not be new but I did just discover, is on that same page where we access the settings and our profile there is this “Recents” tab where you can go and see all of the songs you’ve listened to and the amount of times I’ve used it to find a song I liked in someone else's playlist, or a podcast episode I was listening to but didn’t save, my lord I love that feature so much. But overall it’s a really good app and something that if you get it, it will be used in your daily life if you are a fanatic music listener

Used to be flawless. This app used to be flawless but they keep adding new features that are just frustrating to interact with, especially in situations where you might not have a strong Wi-Fi connection at the time. Most notably, smart shuffle has completely changed the binary operation of shuffle/unshuffle. What they need to do is decouple this functionality from the existing shuffle and make it a toggleable feature. The problems with smart shuffle are numerous, but I’ll just name the main ones I can think of 

Smart shuffle being enabled then disabled by cycling through the shuffle button’s operations interrupts your currently selected play schedule. For example. If I search my liked songs for an album and I want to just play the album in order, I can’t just simply tap on the song I want to start from and have the album loop unless the last thing I was listening to was unshuffled as well. If the last thing you listened to was shuffled, it remembers that option. So then I have to go and unshuffle (which turns on smart shuffle) and then turn off smart shuffle. If you are on cellular data, this process takes like 10-30 seconds.  This process seems to break the existing play schedule that I had queued up, and I am then forced to go and search again for what I want to listen to and then tap the song I want to start from. 

The fact that this feature is not optional, yet adds frustrating workarounds and limitations just to retain pre-existing functionality is a disgrace to standard app design practices

The lyrics were introduced years ago and still there are songs that have to wait very long for the lyrics or have none at all I find it rediculous that a full scale genius powered lyrics feature has not been introduced at this point when Apple Music has been ahead with this feature for years now. Another issue is with shuffle it has increasingly gotten worse over the years but now it makes listening to your playlist almost unbearable. You will listen to an artist and then 3 more songs from that said artist will pop up back to back to back in the queue. Why is that happening in a playlist with 150+ songs? The other thing that tends to happen often is replaying the same song/songs over and over despite reshuffling or restarting the app. Why am I hearing the same song 3 times before more playlist has played all the way through?? I admit I’m too stubborn to make a switch to Apple Music because this app has been so good for so long but these issues have got to get fixed. Hope to see a change 🙏🏽

Best music app by far but room for a couple of improvements. I’ll start with some new(er) changes I enjoyed. I like how when i click on my profile in the top left the menu now provides a better looking UI, easier to get to my settings. Another newish thing I loved is when i add a new song to a playlist, it’s a check the playlists instead of only being able to add it to one playlist at a time, definitely a time saver. 
     Now for some new tweaks. 
I would like to see more customization with the quick swiping right or left on a song. There should be a setting to be able to change what a swipe left or a swipe right means instead of it always being queuing a song or liking it.
The other feature I would like added is to be able to change the order on how your playlists display on your public profile. Let’s be honest, there are definitely better playlists that are created than others. I feel like this could be done two different ways. A: give the user full customization of the order, or B: allow the user to pin 3-4 playlists on their public profile sort of how the pin feature works for your playlists in your personal music library.
     Thank you for your time and for the improvements you constantly make

I guess I’m switching to a different music app for now, where I am actually able to pick the music I listen to instead of an algorithm doing it for me

Here’s Why You Should Use YT Music Instead of This Scam Platform. This app used to be top of the world but I am constantly met with new growing issues for the free listening experience. First off: THIS IS NOT A FREE APP. It’s ok still to use this on desktop, but the mobile version does not let you listen to what you want. For music, I made myself lists obviously, but what’s the point if I can’t even listen to it? Shuffle is locked behind premium, choice is locked behind premium, and you’re entire playlist after song one is locked behind premium. Instead, after your first song, they will shuffle based on what they THINK you might like, which is almost never right and you have limited skips due to ANOTHER locked premium feature. Then there’s loop that basically won’t work with premium and only a limited amount of lyrics without premium… when you could just see those online if you wanted. Essentially they expect you to be stupid enough to pay for convenience. So why don’t I leave? Because they pay for exclusive rights to certain podcasts, and I can’t view them elsewhere. 🙄
They have started poaching creators too from other social medias for exclusive rights and it’s awful. Additionally you don’t get to customize your podcasts section (EVEN WHEN I HAD PREMIUM) and now I’m stuck with two recommendations I NEVER EVEN WANTED. Some stupid car podcast and cryptid podcast (two topics I don’t even remotely care about).
If I can sum this up… it’s corporate greed and transactions disguised as “free”

Premium is perfect for all the music lovers!. This app is awesome with the premium! You can make your own playlists of all the music you love, shuffle, repeat albums or one certain song and the absence of adds is awesome! You can pick and chose which songs you want to listen to and... it’s just awesome! There’s so much about it that I love! The only bummer I’ve found with it, is that some of my most favorite soundtracks or songs aren’t available as in they don’t show up when you search them (yes there is a search, so awesome!). I’m not sure why, but the Final Fantasy XV soundtrack isn’t on there, and FFXV Kingsglaive isn’t there either. It could be for reasons I just don’t know about, but I’m really bummed about that. I would love to see it added in if it’s possible. One other bummer is that they might have the soundtrack for the first season of a TV series, but they don’t have any of the others or all of them (with Star Wars Rebels or Voltron Legendary Defenders). Those are the only two bummers I have with the app, and they are minor things. The shows and soundtracks I mentioned are the only ones I’ve seen so far, and the variety of all the different music you can listen to is huge. Other than that, it is awesome! Another cool thing I would like to mention is the download option where you can download an entire album and listen to it off WYFY, which is great for road trips or poor connection areas! Anyways, I love this app, won’t mind some more soundtrack albums to be added like Final Fantasy XV, Voltron and Star Wars Rebels, but other than that, it’s perfect for all those music lovers!

Unnecessary Updates Lowered Quality of Life. Big changes were made to how the shuffle system works in the app. This would be fine if they had left the option of still maintaining the original shuffle system, but instead, now whenever I’d like to shuffle my massive playlists for a new set of songs, not only does it not shuffle the current song that went on first, I also can’t hit go and hit a single button to do the shuffle for me. Instead, I have to turn off the shuffle mode, turn it back on, specify that I don’t want their unnecessary “smart” shuffle mode that plays music outside of my playlist (just why?), and then check what new batch of songs it chose to grant me while still not changing the first song it decided to hit me with. OR I can hit play on my playlist with shuffle on, exit that playlist, hit play on ANOTHER playlist, and then go back to the first to hit play again. It makes no sense why they’d remove this feature. At all. It’s super inconvenient. Especially since if you wanted to play through an album or playlist without shuffle (just to listen from front to back as the artist intended) all you had to do was tap off the shuffle button and press the top song in the list. An easy two-step process that takes less than a second or two. But for shuffling playlists and playing roulette with your music when you’re still trying to figure out your vibe takes upwards of three to six seconds every single time. There was no need for this. And this trend has been notably ongoing for a couple of years now. Simply irritating. 
Audio and streaming are decently good, only reason it’s not a 1 Star

Please fix what once was a good app. This app is constantly laggy and buggy to an obnoxious degree. For example, if I have the app open and then I connect my Bluetooth headphones, it freezes for a good 5-10 seconds before I can actually do anything. If I have the app open for a while and then I stop listening to music and then go back in, it shuts off the equalizer. But it doesn’t physically turn it off, it just completely bypasses the setting and gives me a flat sounding stream until I go in and turn the EQ off and then back on. Speaking of settings, they moved its location after years and years of it being in the “your library” tab. Now it is on the “home” tab. A small complaint, but when you’ve spent a long time going to one location for something and they move it, it becomes a nuisance and seems like it’s just a UI designer trying to justify his paycheck over there. And speaking of moving things, they completely removed albums from the bottom of an album page. When you go to an album or even a single, the artists other releases used to show up beneath the current selected album. Now, you have to go to the artists page, and then likely click to see their discography just to get to what you want. They’ve taken a streamlined UI that was so simple to use and complicated it to a mess of multiple pages, making even a simple task a process. Please fix all these incessant problems that somehow escape the seemingly weekly “bug fix” updates and return the UI to something that is actually user friendly so that listening to music is simple and enjoyable again

Music Playlist Feature Request. I’ve been using this app since 2011, I love it. Arguably one of the best features is the recommended plays post listening to a playlist. Great way to discover new music. Problem is, if you hit a point where the music suggested is great, you have to wait for each song to play and actively add each to a playlist to save the songs for listening. If you don’t there’s a chance you’ll lose the song and never loop it into your profile. If you’re doing anything while listening like driving, working, cooking, you have to pause what you’re doing to add the songs in (there’s an add in there for product rollout - hire me for the full PM). If the app notified you when playlists transitioned into recommended songs, and gave you the option to create a playlist of the future suggested songs for a certain amount of time, say 30 min, 2 hours, remainder of work day, etc., with option to stop before if music turns, it would make expanding a music profile way easier and automate the process of making playlists without disturbing whatever you’re in the middle of doing. Queue roadtrip across America and making an elaborate cake from scratch

Trash customer service. I’ve been a loyal customer for over 10 years, and this month they hit a new low by triple-billing me. To resolve *their* mistake, they canceled my subscription without a second thought however it took 14 hours, despite the fact that I’ve been with them for over a decade. And don’t even get me started on the customer service: I’ve dealt with 20 different reps, each taking at least 10 minutes to respond, leaving me hanging every step of the way. They don’t even have the decency to provide a phone number, just a dysfunctional chat service. One rep even refused to close the conversation so I couldn’t leave feedback on the survey—no doubt because they know how bad they are.

As if that wasn’t enough, the product itself has become a lackluster . The algorithms used to be innovative, now I’m stuck with “custom” playlists that are just the same 40 songs in each one but recycled in different orders. There’s no effort to introduce new music, and the offline feature, which I depend on, rarely works. The website is a buggy mess, making it clear that they’ve stopped caring about the user experience altogether.

To say I’m furious is an understatement. It’s beyond infuriating that a company I’ve supported for over 10 years has fallen this far, and it’s clear they have absolutely no respect for their customers time nor do they care if they retain them

If I choose to “listen on this phone” then the same banner shows up again, but this time the display shows that I am connected to a different iPhone. The process repeats. I have taken numerous screenshots f the is dude with an aim to report the problem, however, there is no in-app mechanism that I can locate which allows me to do so, another application failure. I’ve logged in and out of the app numerous times in an attempt to fix the issue. That doesn’t work. I disabled WiFi and Bluetooth on y phone in an attempt to isolate the issue. The problem still persists even while on cellular service alone. I attempted to change my password in another attempt to fix the issue, however, the app doesn’t allow users to change their password; yet another failure. I deleted the app from my phone and downloaded it again, multiple times (thus ensuring I have the most up-to-date version. This failed to solve the bug. There you have it, my summary of why this is a spectacular failure and meritorious of a single star review.

This seems like a bug... I listen to the Daily Mixes a lot. They’re a quick and easy way to find something to listen to that fits the mood I’m in. Sometimes when I’m listening to one of those mixes, something will play that I love, and I’ll listen to that one track for the rest of the day. Then, the next day, I’ll remember that song but can’t remember the name, so I’ll go to my listening history to try to find the song, but the history will only show that I listened to Daily Mix 4. Yesterday, Daily Mix 4 was hip-hop. Today, Daily Mix 4 is drum & bass, and the song history from the day before isn’t displayed. Or, Daily Mix 4 was drum & bass yesterday, and it still is today, but the songs in the mix are not the same as they were yesterday. Probably the expected behavior for the history feature would be to accurately present a list of every song we’ve listened to. It seems the Daily Mix feature may be creating a problem there. Update: I just noticed that something similar happens if the song is a liked song. The history page only shows that I listened to liked songs. It doesn’t show the actual songs I listened to. Thanks for listening!

Listening History + Personalized mixes. I want to sing praises for the addition of listening history to the mobile app. Please never remove this feature! It’s so useful for discovering new music while doing other things. For example, I can shuffle a playlist of new songs while doing chores, and then go back when I’m done to save the songs I liked. Also good when other people are adding songs to a queue, meaning I would have no way of getting back to the song w/o a history feature. 

My main gripe (only one I can think of) is how personalized playlists— like daily mixes or radio stations— lately have a tendency to change while I’m listening to them. I’ll pick a station based on seeing a good lineup I want to hear, but then as I get partway through, the playlist automatically reshuffles with new songs, and I don’t get to hear what I wanted to. It happens especially if I “like” a new song while listening. 

I get that it’s meant to adapt to your taste dynamically, but it’s annoying when it wipes the playlist you were just listening to (not just the upcoming songs, but the ones you already listened to also). In my opinion it should only update once at the start of each day, the first time you click on it

3 Simple Fixes to Get Back to 5 Stars. 1. You need to include an alphabetical quick scroll on the right-hand side of all of your lists, whether it’s your list of playlists, your Artist Library, or inside an actual playlist looking at songs. No one has the time to swipe for 30 seconds to the bottom of their library when they want to listen to The Who. A quick scroll has been a standard on every music app for years now. Get with the times!

2. The artist library page needs a royal update. Or retro-date, rather. I had an older version of the app until yesterday where all artists whose music you had saved into your library displayed on the same library in a neat, clean fashion, regardless of whether or not you Follow them. Now you can only neatly organize those artists whom you Follow, and the rest are left in this awkward, disorganized list of artists with a big “Follow” button next to it. We know what you’re doing guys. If we want to follow an artist, we will. For now, just let us organize our artist library in a straightforward fashion.

3. Another retro-date: put back the option to view your Songs Library. Sometimes you just wanna shuffle your songs. Yeah, the “Liked Songs” playlist is there, and that’s cool and all, but it’s not intuitive when you have “Playlists” and “Artists” listed up top. Put it back

Confusing and awful update. Never written a review for any app before but doing one for the update now because I want to play my part in having the app changed back to what it was before so that I don’t have to switch platforms. The latest update didn’t improve anything at all but made accessing your music - which is the whole point of the app - extremely more difficult and inconvenient. Firstly, now you can only access artists in your library from which you have saved songs if you are also following them, thereby forcing you to follow any artists if you want to be able to easily access songs you have saved by them in the artist category of your library. I saw someone complain about how this is nearly forcing your music listening - something for me which is a very solitary thing - into being a social media outlet; I agree with that and find it very annoying. Secondly, if you want to have an album accessible in your library to listen to, you need to have liked all of the songs on the album in order for it to appear there. This means that if you have liked 9 out of 10 songs on an album, and you just want to listen to those songs which you have liked, you can’t; instead, you need to like that one extra song in order for the album to appear in your library, and then you can’t easily play through just the songs you like. I really have zero clue why they did this. Overall, the app before was by no means great, but this update in retrospect makes the old UI seem wonderful. Don’t update if you haven’t yet - you will be saving yourself unnecessary inconveniences by doing so

It’s gotten worse and worse…. I started using this app in 2017. It used to be pretty decent, the ad breaks weren’t overbearing or frequent, and you could actually watch an ad for 30 minutes with no ads. I liked that. As time went on… well… more ads, no more 30 minute uninterrupted music, and consistently broken features. Now it’s gotten to the point where there is an ad after every single song. I understand you have to get paid but Jesus, that’s too much. And now things are constantly broken. Recently it’s been an issue with things I’ve liked (songs and albums both) as well as my playlist, which is the ONLY reason I still use this godforsaken app. I have a 190hr metal playlist, over 2,500 songs, can’t replace that without a ton of work. But as I said, songs I’ve liked have been showing up as unliked when played, but you can go to liked songs and they’re there. Same with albums, it shows I haven’t liked an album but I can go to the albums tab and see it. It’s been taking songs out of my playlist too, for some reason. Songs that were there are now disappearing and I have to go re-add them. Albums have also been mysteriously disappearing off of artist pages. I noticed a Warbringer single (Firepower Kills specifically) has suddenly vanished off the app altogether, don’t know if that’s an artist removal thing or what. And yea, I can still listen to the version on the Weapons of Tomorrow album, but it’s odd the single version of just that song has vanished. Moral of the story, avoid this app altogether unless you’re like me with a massive playlist that would take eons to replace elsewhere

However, the more that podcasts are moving to paid content the more my feed is being taken over by bonus episodes and the most annoying part is that you can’t get rid of them. Normally every morning I go through my feed and mark stuff I am not interested in listening to as played so it goes away. Then I just have a lot of episodes I plan to listen to. Not anymore! Please allow users to hide or mark those episodes as played. I can’t help but think this is really just a form of advertising because they want you to be tempted by those episodes. The one other complaint I have is that trying to listen from my iwatch is just not worth the hassle. It doesn't work half the time even if I download the episode so I've started just using apple podcasts when I walk the dog without my phone

Pretty good but.... It’s a pretty good app, you can get a lot of music and make your own playlists so that’s nice. It also has good sound quality and does it’s best to stabilize sound so you don’t get really jarred by the start of a new song. 
My complaints are that there is a bug that just stops your music even when your internet connection is great. It often happens when your phone screen is locked, and sometimes when you open your phone and go to the app it has completely crashed, other times it has just paused it for some reason.
 My other complaint is more trivial, and it is about the auto play of recommended songs. I understand that the app is designed to act like a radio station, where you can’t really choose which songs you play and some will be played that are relating to the genre. However, I just really hate this feature, especially when I am playing songs from a artist that I really like however it is in a genre that I generally hate. Ideally the recommend song would be close enough to the artist’s style that the user might like the song as well even though they don’t like the genre, however that hasn’t been the case yet for me.

Other than my two complaints, this app is pretty great and I recommend it

After over a decade…. I have finally canceled my premium subscription. They’re doing to much, trying to make into a social media platform instead of just focusing on a place for music. And what’s with the algorithm? The mixes only give you repeat music and so does the DJ… I hate that — if I wanted to listen to all my favorites songs that’s what I would do! If I wanted random mixes made for me, it should have a few select favorites and mostly all new to help me discover new songs and new artists. But it’s been years of this now. It all kind of went down hill after they got rid of the like or dislike feature, which they should bring back because that system served its purpose. Now, while they did give us plenty of cool updates on mobile like able to add folders and add photos to our playlists. They took away the ability to easily reorganize our playlists on pc. I had thousands of playlists that I used to be able to move around with out a problem, newer playlists could be moved down and buried and old ones could be brought up and moved towards the top, and that’s just not really a thing anymore. Everything is becoming so limited - while they keep adding new things. It just doesn’t serve its purpose, it used to be a place to perfectly craft new playlists and discover new music. But now it seems like I’m being forced to fed the same things. Anyway, it’s been nice. I’m either going back to an iPod or will try to find an app that has better discover features. Thanks for everything! Can’t keep paying for what I’m getting! I also think it’s really is not  to force people to log in with codes instead of passwords

Shuffle play only for people with the free plan... really?!?!!. I really do like this app and it’s cool music but I have one problem with it. People that don’t have a premium membership (have a free plan) are only able to play songs by shuffle playing. You use to be able to choose whatever song you would want to listen to on your playlist and you could shuffle play too but now you are only able to shuffle play if you don’t have the premium membership, and also, when I tap on the list of all of my songs in my playlist and I find a song that I want to play, it only plays a preview of the song. I think the shuffle play is kinda dumb because whenever I shuffle play it and my last song on my playlist stops playing (because the song is over) it moves on to a new song that I haven’t added to my playlist and whenever I hit the button that says “Shuffle Play” it controls if the song is paused or playing. I tried exiting out and reopening the app but the song is still there and acts as if it’s in my playlist.
I would give this app 5 stars if it weren’t for the shuffle play only thing.
Please change it so that people that don’t have a premium membership can listen to whatever songs they’d like too. Thanks for listen and have a good day

Eh.  Please stop changing layouts and button locations!. Been paying for premium for a few years (they got to me with their Wakanda ads every other song) and while its handy for quickly accessing the songs you want, the in-app design and layout of buttons and features offered continue to change with their too-frequent updates.  It gets really annoying.  And the shuffle button is now nonintuitive with no insight into what particular shuffle you’re switching to.  Wouldn’t matter to learn it as they’ll update it again next week and change that again as well.  Updates are just to keep the app high on the download list, and to tweak things that don’t need tweaking.  What does need tweaking is your shuffle actually shuffling beyond your top played songs. Please stop shifting things around and tweaking existing features!  Might be time to change apps, the price isn’t worth the fuss and repetition of the same songs.  Also would be handy to be able to create a station that doesn’t bring my existing songs into it.  Went on a vacation recently and couldn’t make a station for the family to hear without my songs.  They just want everyone to pay for premium.  My advice, find another app.  The artists don’t make anything with this one

Complicated. Please read this.. This is getting extremely irritating. New update is horrible. I had no issues with this app until the new UI update. I don’t care for the car mode. I liked being able to see the album artwork. When I plug in my phone to the car to charge it, it changes the audio playback from Bluetooth to “docking station” and stops playing from the speakers. I have to manually go in the settings and select my car Bluetooth. Absolutely annoying. I just want to charge my phone, it’s not a docking station!
I never write reviews but this update is so horrible I just had to. The lastest thing I discovered that irked me is that after being disconnected from my car Bluetooth, the app continues to play rather than stop as it used to. I haven’t used the app thoroughly yet unlike some other reviewers but I’m sure I’d find more things to not like

90% amazing! Now about that 10%…. Like the title suggests, I REALLY like this app. It’s amazing and I can listen to SO MANY SONGS! But there are a few complaints I have. For starters, the adds. Now unlike most apps that just slap you with an add every 10 seconds your app doesn’t! Which I am really thankful for. However… my one issue is you literally have adds ADVERTISING TO GET RID OF ADDS! its ok but needs some major improvements. its nice to listen to music for free on this app however if you dont pay for premium you cant do much at all except make playlists, and shuffle. also the ads are insufferably annoying. the ads play way too often, it seems like every three songs  four 30 second ads play, and they're extremely loud too, i like listening to relaxing music at night to go to sleep and hearing an ad about jp morgan at 2 am at full volume is the opposite of calming. this app is cool but with some minor tweaks it could be very very good. First, instead of 6 skips an hour maybe we could have ten. sometimes whats playing on a playlist isnt what you want to hear at a certain time and the majority of the time 6 skips wont do enough to find something else. second, we should be able to restart and scroll through the songs. its ridiculous that we cant do this its such a basic feature thats being stripper from us its terrible. second is less ads. i understand that a free service needs to monetize itself but sometimes i dont even want to use the app because of the frequency of the ads. and the volume once again is way too loud. third, the algorithm is very very bad as well. i personally listen to some less than mainstream sounding music so when i have a song by dababy of justin beiber being recommended to me i obviously am not going to enjoy as much as i might like finding out about a different indie band, or an underground rapper. i feel like with some of these major changes this app could become one of if not the best streaming platform for music but at this point in time its very far from that in my opinion

Love it but. But every single time I’m in the app and I do this It always says error something went wrong. I don’t know what update they did because the past 6 to 8 months it is not worked. The only way you can get your listening hours if you go onto their website. To me this is a huge flaw. 

Furthermore, it goes based off how many hours of the book you’ve listened to. Not to how fast you’ve listened to it, which would actually be how many hours you’ve listened to. I listen to my books at about 1.5 speed, which means I will finish books in a shorter amount of time, meaning I could listen to more books within the 15 hrs. However, that is not the case. Then it is not clear if you go back and listen to a part again if that comes out of your listening time. Even though you just went back in the same book. If this is the case it is incredibly unfair to their customers. More transparency and consistency around audiobooks would be appreciated

Shuffle Play. I love the app and it is my go to for music but I have to say that your guys shuffle play is broke. The reason I know is that I have a personal playlist I created that is almost 40 hours long. I drive trucks for a living so I have long periods of time that I can listen to music everyday. Hypothetically, I should be able to listen to this playlist I created for 10 days straight without hearing the same song again but often when I come back to play it again after a break I will hear the same songs often repeated in the shuffle play the next day. I’m not sure why that is but it needs to be fixed for me to enjoy the playlist I created without hearing a lot of the same songs come up again and again day after day. 

Also, when you finish listening to an album and it starts playing “recommended tracks” based upon what you just heard, please don’t disable the repeat button. Sometimes it will suggest a track that I really like and want to put on repeat but the system won’t allow me to. 

If you guys are able to fix these problems I would really appreciate it! Thanks

Amazing amount of free music; could use some work on "made for you" playlists. It's very easy to use, and has literally every song I've tried to find (including some extremely obscure ones). I only have two complaints. 
One is that in the playlists that the app creates for you, it tends to only play songs that you have already listened to multiple times (in shuffle lol) or popular, similar ones. If I follow an artist, there should be an option to mix their more obscure music with the popular stuff. If a playlist is "made for me," it should be able to play whatever it is I like, whether it's a superstar's most popular hit songs, or the most obscure songs released by a guy with 500 listeners. It's not a radio station; it only has to cater to the tastes of one specific person.
Secondly, there should be a way to add, if not entire artists, then at least full albums to playlists that you're creating from the app. I follow over fifty artists, and if I like all of their music, I don't want to add all 3000+ of their songs individually. It wouldn't be a hard feature to add, and would make me actually likely to create a playlist or two.
Thank you for reading my review!

Latest Updates Not User Friendly for Playlists. The most recent updates don’t allow you to see which songs you’ve previously liked on a playlist. Makes it harder to discover new songs because even if I heard a song and liked it on the app, I’d have no way of remembering that I’ve heard and liked the song previously unless I knew the song by name and could look it up or found it in my “Liked” list (unlikely to recall the name of any newly discovered song among the thousands of songs in my “Liked” list.)

The heart function. Why on god’s green earth would you get rid of the hearts that denote whether a song is liked. Now, I look at albums and can’t even see which songs I’ve liked. Why would you take the heart, synonymous with the human condition, indicative of jubilation and love, and replace it with an ineffective, transparent, and frankly depressing plus sign and check. I just want to know what songs I’ve already added to playlists or liked with a glance, to not have to take extra steps just to find out what I’ve done to a song. Why must a simple, effective, and beloved feature be removed, for the sake of change? Change may be a constant of life, but consistency is a part of the everyday experience as well. Every time I need to press extra buttons and pop-ups to remember if I’ve liked a song or not, I can feel my outlook on life fading from technicolor to black and white. Every time I have to physically add a song to “liked songs,” my stomach churns. I know that whoever proposed this idea, and all those who approved it, will suffer in the afterlife with punishment that would make sysiphus shudder. I genuinely cannot wrap my head around the decision. I cannot imagine the thought process behind depriving users of the little joys in life, the heart in this case. The only possible conclusion is that the decision must have been made by someone who lacks true delight, because anyone with an iota of a happy life would have never let this come to fruition. Alas, I am but a humble consumer. I use what little power I am granted to bestow a 1 star rating. One can hope that a shout into the abyss will be heard, but I do not know if I am the kind to hope

(ICE) Ads - REALLY?!. I canceled my streaming subscriptions after experiencing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) ads on platforms I trusted.
These ads are a harmful misuse of mainstream streaming channels. ICE’s enforcement-focused messaging promotes fear, dehumanizes immigrants, and normalizes surveillance and detention policies that cause real suffering for families and communities. Streaming services should not amplify a federal enforcement agency’s political and coercive messages alongside entertainment and news. Doing so erodes public trust, turns leisure platforms into instruments of state power, and signals that profit and ad revenue trump ethical responsibility.
For viewers who expect safe, neutral spaces in their homes, these ads cross a line. I refuse to support platforms that accept money to broadcast messaging from agencies whose actions I find morally objectionable. Effective, responsible platforms should reject ads that contribute to harm or stigmatize vulnerable populations.
I’ve canceled my subscriptions and encourage others who disagree with this practice to do the same or to demand transparent ad policies that prohibit content from agencies or organizations whose activities undermine human rights and public trust. Platforms can and should prioritize ethics over ad revenue — until they do, I won’t be paying to fund messaging that harms people

Very good, however there is a thing or two that are odd. This app is very good! Extremely easy to navigate, and has a massive amount of songs to choose from(even covers made by fans), however there is a thing or two that brings some questions to mind.
1. Phone vs computer/iPad abilities
Whenever an ad says mobile, it really means phones specifically.  My iPad has all of the same abilities as my computer(such as choosing whatever song I wish without premium, unlimited skips, etc), however phones only allow you to select the playlist and have a limited skip number. Premium makes all of that go away, of course, but not everyone can spend that money every month just to listen to one specific song every once in a while.
2. Shuffle
When you select the shuffle button, it doesn’t truly randomize the songs you’re listening to. The less you skip songs or remove them from the queue, the more likely those songs will pop up. Likewise, if you skip songs, they will come up less. I personally don’t have a problem with it, as all algorithms can be trained, however it isn’t exactly a true shuffle like one would first assume.
3. Liked playlist customization
This is purely cosmetic, but I would love to be able to change the colors on my liked playlist cover(or even the image!). It’s simple, but it makes a massive difference when it comes to how much people enjoy the app. Many people don’t wanna look at their least favorite colors when they open up their playlist of favorite songs, essentially. It ruins the vibe

Frustrating. I used to be able to find new music through Spotify’s suggestions and made for you playlists. Now I struggle to get anything but the same music I already have in several playlists and music I’m not interested in at all. And hiding those is useless because inevitably, the same songs or similar will end up on other playlists or suggestions. Gettin g rid of the dislike button made this app a nightmare. I can’t just say ‘nope, don’t like this song, don’t play it again’ like I used to be able to do or like you could do on Pandora. No, you get the option to hide the song on that particular playlist, and you can only use the hide option if you’re on the mobile app, so good luck desktop app users. There are days when my daily mixes or daylists are unlistenable because they’re exclusively music I don’t like. So yeah, the algorithm is doing great analyzing my music tastes. And hiding artists only seems to work on mobile and doesn’t travel over to the desktop app either. Bring back the dislike button and actually let it impact music suggestions! It makes no sense to be able to like music but not be able to dislike it to better fine tune the suggestions!

Can’t restart playlist. So, the app is great, yeah. Good music, doesn’t drain my battery like some other music apps. However, the user interface is pretty bad, meaning you can’t restart your playlist. Once it’s done with your songs, it plays some recommended songs, aka songs that aren’t in my playlist, aka songs I don’t want to listen to. I know some people appreciate this feature, but not me. I only want to listen to the songs in my playlist and nothing but, however there is no way I can restart my playlist. I’ve googled it and everything to no avail. I’ve seen people say “there is no easy way to restart your playlist” but I can’t find any way whatsoever. Please, just please change this so I don’t have to use some other bad app that crashes every three songs. Also, what’s the point of shuffling when it plays your songs in the same order anyway? Yeah, it plays it in a random order, but it’s that same random order every time. Mix it up! Overall, good as an app, good idea, bad execution

Another app torn apart by the greed of massive corporations. :). There’s an unbelievable amount of ads, first of all. I mean every 2-3 songs, there are around five ads. Free version is: Pretty bad (If you’re planning on paying the premium fee, then move on). Some of you happy review-readers might be thinking: “Well, of course the free version is bad. It’s the free version!” You are right. However, take this in consideration: 1) Why, why, why would I ever want a bunch of songs that I “might like” show up on my playlist instead of the songs that I actually put on my playlist? 2) There should be a play button for the regular shuffle function, AND a separate play button for when you want to continue the playlist. This goes back to the unwanted songs that are tacked onto a playlist. No matter what, it seems that one cannot reset the playlist unless they desire the unholy wait time of a whole day. 3) 6 skips is pretty skimpy. Now, if the only songs that showed up in your playlists were the ones you put in there, than I would be fine with it. BUT, when I have to skip through 6 suggested songs that I had no intention of listening to, that “I’m fine with it” turns into “I want to swallow straight horse dung for 24 hours.”
Have I any problems with the premium version? …..No, other than the fact that it’s not free

Great app but.... It’s a great app to listen to music with all of the choices. There’s a lot of pros and cons about this. Newer coaching. I’ve coached rec ball for the last three years and I see what I’ve done wrong and right by listening to this podcast. Starting off, I was told by a really respected coach in my area that I only had one great player. He told me work her to death and forget about the rest. I really thought about that moment long and hard. The more I thought about it the more it irritated me. Yes I took a team that no one drafted because it was daughters first year and she didn’t make it on the “real” other teams. It was a rough year with one win but the girls all pulled it together as a team. Now looking back, I never put them down for a mistake but only pushed them harder. When they were down I would do whatever I could to lift them up. The next year I had my most regrets. I picked my own team and we dominated, but like you said I was in it to win it. A couple girls didn’t come back after that year because I left them in the outfield and never gave them a chance. I will definitely keep on listening, thanks for the great words

I usually get around three-four ADs, and when these ADs end, usually three songs play. However, sometimes I get the struggle of only getting one song to play then two or three advertisements play. Inconvenient. Great music player and I would definitely recommend it, if they didn’t make so difficult to play a single song. If you want to play just one song, chances are, it will play a totally different song that you didn’t want to hear. It has a feature called “extra songs” that add recommended songs to you, which wouldn’t be a problem if they didn’t play those songs BEFORE the song you wanted to listen to in the first place. They also add extra songs to your playlists. There is no work around this issue. You can stop all the extra songs one at a time from playing, but it takes forever and if it’s a song from one of your other playlists it’ll delete the song from that playlist as well. You can skip the song, but you only have six skips a day unless you get the premium version, which wouldn’t be a problem except for the fact that you can’t get the premium on the app. Which eliminates the entire point of this app...convenience. This problem would be SO easy to fix, but they deliberately try to make the app inconvenient so you’ll buy the premium version that you can’t get on the app! If you’re looking for a good music player, use ITUNES

I love this app, just need more changes to be perfect. Pro:
1. I love this app a lot. It’s very convenient to use. It allows me the option to listen on different devices as long as they are on the same WiFi. Also the playlists are pretty good, and I especially like that when a certain playlist finishes, it continues to play lay songs similar to the ones in the playlist. This allows me to discover more songs that I would most likely like a lot. 

Con: 
1. But there are few areas that I would like to be improved. In particular, when making my own playlists. I love making different workout playlist so that I am not constantly playing the same set of songs shuffle during my workout. It makes me exhausted and gets me bored of songs I especially like really quick. The only issue is that sometimes I may already have a song one playlist, but accidentally add it to a a new playlist as well. By doing so, this defeats the purpose of different playlists since the songs in them are the same. So if it’s possible for the app to let me know that when I add so-so song to a playlist 1)is this song already in that playlist (think it already does this) and 2) is this song in another playlist.

2. Another area that could be improved is the shuffling of the songs. I know it says it’s supposed to be random but why so I feel this is not the case. For example, when I am on one playlist, despite having shuffle on, I still get the same songs (even if there might be 50 songs in it) in the first 30mins? I wish this could be improved so I can ever guess which song will play first or second

Update is abysmal.. I blew a thick layer of dust off of the iTunes app icon so I could come leave this review.  The new interface is stripped of some of the most useful features that it has had for years!  This complaint is basically all about the "My Library" section. The old versions allowed you to browse your library from more ways than this new version.  The "Albums" view of one's library used to show small icons for the art, and could fit many choices on the screen simultaneously.  The new one takes up so much space with the artwork, so you can only see 4.5 albums EVER (on an iPhone 5).  They also got rid of the quick-alphabet scrolling on the far right of the screen, so you can quickly zoom to any section of the alphabet for browsing. To any big music fan who has hundreds of albums saved, this is simply not a good way to browse! Yes, there's a search function, but in the entire history of recorded music, rifiling through all things in one's collection before knowing what you're in the mood for is an essential part of the music experience.  I tried to instead browse my collection in Artist view, but when you click an artist, you just get ALL of their stuff in one big list, instead of getting to see what album(s) you're looking at.  Also not good. 

Further, the old version showed your "recently played" stuff in an extensive list below the browsing options in "My Libary".  That was a feature I used constantly, because at any given moment, you're probably in the mood for one of the last several dozen things you listened to.  I know there's still the recently played section on Home, but those sections keep scrambling and it isn't a good way to quickly scroll through content. 

PLEASE change it back.  "If it ain't broke, don't fix it!"

Some issues with podcast streaming. I mostly enjoy using this app, however, I’ve noticed several issues over the years with listening to podcast episodes back-to-back. The app does not always save your preference for sorting the podcast episodes, which must be set per show. I usually sort mine Oldest to Newest, with only the unfinished episodes shown to help me keep track of where I am, but half the times I pull up the show I want to listen to, the list has reset to the default- All Episodes, Newest to Oldest. Every time, I open the app and start playing a song after a period of thirty minutes to an hour, the app closes and the song stops playing. So I restart the app and the song. Timing out?. This app was great up until a few updates ago. I’ve held off on posting because I was hoping that te next update or the next would fix the problem I’m having. I have to physically go into the app and restart the music. It’s becoming very frustrating to have to go into the app almost every time I pause my music.

Update: the timing out problem has been worse and worse!!! More often than not, when I pause, I have to go back into the app to let it reload. Something else it’s started doing within the last few updates, is sometimes, after it times out a couple times, it will show the time on a song counting up, but the song will not be playing. At this point, the only reason I’m still paying $9.99 a month for this is because I don’t have reliable WiFi when I’m not home, and I like to listen while I’m out and about

And you can’t purchase more time, you just have to wait so now I’m in the middle of a book, and I can’t finish it until my audiobook time renews. That just doesn’t make sense to me. New updates bad! Bring back useful features!. The “heart icon” is gone so you can no longer know which songs you have saved in your library. This was IMPORTANT when browsing through various albums and playlists to know which songs you already have liked. I’m constantly adding songs with the new “plus” icon to find out I already have them saved to my library. :( There is no longer the option to “like all songs” from an album or playlist, you now have to “add to playlist” > “liked songs”. It’s a change. Will take time to adjust. 
Furthermore removing liked songs with the new plus icon takes an additional step, as you have to then select if you want to uncheck if from liked songs or add it to additional playlists. Convenient if you want to add a song to multiple playlists at once, but extra step to remove from liked songs. Though it slows down accidental un-liking of songs.

Driving mode anti-safety. Great app. Driving mode can be a real hassle though. Driving mode is inconvenient, unconventional and more of a hassle than what it’s worth. It’s a little ridiculous to assume what your customers need for their own safety while potentially creating more problems for them

Great App!. I immediately feel in love into this app when I got it! The pros of this app are that the large variety of songs and artist, which is spectacular! I also enjoy listening the fact that you can save as many songs as you'd like. The fact that you are allowed to have the free trial for premium is magnificent, but I have not tried it yet. I also like the fact that you can watch an ad to get rid of advertisements for a half an hour, so I can listen to music without interruptions.
  
     However, there are quite a few cons. First, you can't listen to a thirty second preview of new songs, which saved lots of time. Then, there's also the fact that unsaved songs of yours can randomly start playing, which is great for discovering some new songs and artist, but gets irritating when I want to listen to my songs. 

       Other than that, this app is great for playing any song while out of the app, and I'm sure I will still be a user in the future! Keep improving the app to make it better and better!

And for recommendations, it doesn’t seem to understand the massive difference between older rap like 50 Cent and stuff like Kanye. Whenever I use smart shuffle on my gangster rap playlist it always lines classics up with Drake and 21. They’re not even remotely similar and no matter how many songs I remove from recommended or how far from that type of rap I try to make my playlist it always mixes in that stuff and it kills the vibe. Not only for rap though, on my classic rock playlists (nothing newer than the 80’s) I’ll turn on recommended and get songs from Shinedown, Three Days Grace, and Korn. I just wish it was better at reading the generation of music in a playlist

Met an Ologite!. Hey Aly!
Just wanted to tell you a cute story about how you bring people together! So, earlier this week I was at the car wash and the Subaru Outback in front of me had a vanity license plate of “Ologite”. So, as I progressed through the wash cycle I was imagining a fun way to ask her if she was a fellow fan. Honestly, I almost chickened out. Instead, I hiked up my big girl pants and approached her. We were both so happy with our little encounter. Honestly, I didn’t even catch her name because of the car vacuums that were blaring during our conversation. I did make out that she loves your show and that she has gone back to school to study marine biology. We hugged and she told me that I was the best part of her day! Later as I was wiping down my car I wholeheartedly shared her sentiment. 
So thank you for the chance meeting that you caused and thank you to my unknown “Ologite”. I hope to one day have a conversation with you without the deafening vacuum!

New UI is unusable. I don’t know what possessed the developers to make any of the changes they did, but it’s rendered the app almost useless. All my liked songs/albums are now tossed together into a single unholy genre coleslaw of a playlist that I can neither organize, nor remove, nor move from the top of my library where I have to look at the useless bastard at all times. For some reason they’ve also moved the recently played list, which I used constantly due to it being accessible offline and a convenient way to jump back into whatever I’ve played recently, to the homepage, which I never used, and which inexplicably needs an internet connection, which I frequently don’t have, and disabled the ability to remove things from it. Sometimes it doesn’t show up at all and the only thing they have to offer is playlists they’ve made based on whatever I used to play ten years ago during my ill advised teenage emo phase. Or podcast suggestions, which is not helpful considering I don’t listen to podcasts via this app. They also made it scroll sideways for some reason, but honestly given everything else that’s a minor problem that I can only attribute to stupidity rather than active malice. But, you know, iPhones have been around for a while, surely we all know they have vertical screens by now. Or not. I won’t assume at this point that a single person involved here knows anything about iPhones or apps.

If the devs respond to this with some patronizing copy/pasted response about how much they value feedback and want to improve the app for their users I swear to god I will delete the app and my subscription on the spot and find some other option. If anyone involved actually cared what the user base thought you would have reverted the changes by now, you’ve had months to figure this out

Well done and very thought provoking.. My daughter introduced me to the Exorcist Files. Father Martins has opened our eyes to some of the things that we had believed to be innocent or helpful in the paranormal shows. We are talking about God, angels, psychic mediums, magic, and demons in a way that is building us up spiritually and helping us to grow as Christians. Still good, but not as great. While I can still recommend this app for the most part, the new update makes it rather frustrating to navigate. 

Songs are no longer organized to their own section, and gone is the alphabet scroll bar. I used to enjoy scrolling through and finding whatever struck my fancy, but the new update forces liked songs into a playlist which, understandably, functions as a playlist. This makes it difficult to get to the lower sections, and either forces the user to just shuffle it, know all the songs by heart, or settle for playing the upper most songs without considerable effort.

A second gripe- no longer able to search my artists or albums. Artists are only the ones followed, but if I want to play songs only by a certain artist, I’ve yet to find a way to be able to short of making another playlist. Album’s too are no longer listed, it seems to be only the ones where the user follows the artist who made the album in question.

The new podcast look is no problem, and the division of music and podcasts into two adjacent sections is a pretty good play. However, not if it forces the music section to be less user friendly and in turn force it to be less functional.

If the new and old versions could be married somehow, where music is just as navigable, that’d be great. But as it currently stands, this new update seems to have forced up a new feature and the expense of some very practical features, and it’s a bummer the app is no longer quite as user friendly

Generally love, but lacking a few key things. 1. You need a place where users can easily send anonymous feedback on the app or at least your website. I hate having to do so in a review, but oh well!
2. PLEASE make songs deletable from play lists by clicking the ellipses menu button on the song (much like you would to add it  to a Playlist in the first place). With no search function in the "edit" view of your Playlists, and no great way to organize them pre-edit on the phone app, it is a PAIN to delete songs from long Playlists. Similarly, would be nice to be able to add 1 song to multiple Playlists by having a multi-select option instead of having to go back and click the "add to Playlist" button multiple times.
3. Folders would be a better way to be able to organize similar Playlists together in the app for easier browsing of my own saved favorites 
4. Would be nice if the daily mixes were more consistent with the number and general genre, or if you could save ones you really liked. I originally tried to make daily mix 6 my alarm clock every morning but soon realized it doesn't always have the hype-up jams I need to get out of bed at 430am!
5. The search results need to have more options for sorting. Random play lists show up first that haven't been edited in over a year. Should be able to sort by things like "most recently updated" or "most followers", etc
6. Radio stations are still better on Pandora. This is generally improving since 2013 (kudos for that!) but still missing the mark on things like artist variety and avoiding multiple repeats

Great… but there’s glitches. Overall, the app is fantastic—I love the playlists, music selection, and general functionality. However, there are two frustrating issues that have been driving me crazy lately.
	1.	Adding Music Errors: Often, when I try to add a song, I get a “something went wrong” error, and the song doesn’t get added. I’ve lost so many tracks because of this, which is incredibly frustrating.
	2.	Smart Shuffle Repeating Patterns: The “smart shuffle” feature seems to get stuck in a loop. When I shuffle my liked songs, the app generates a preset order—but no matter what I do, it keeps playing the same songs in the exact same sequence. Even if I switch playlists and come back, it still plays in the same order. This is especially annoying when I’m on the go and expect a fresh shuffle, only to get the same sequence over and over. I often have to stop what I’m doing and manually toggle shuffle settings, which doesn’t always fix the issue.

If these issues were resolved, the experience would be near perfect!

Some annoying glitches. Overall the app has some cool features, but there are a couple glitches that prevent a five star rating. First, if you’re connected to a Bluetooth device, you cannot remove podcasts from a playlist, nor can you remove podcast downloads from your phone. Doesn’t make sense that I should have to  disconnect from external devices to use normal functions within the app. Second, concerning podcasts, I created a playlist of one of my favorite podcasts because I want to listen to only a few select shows. Instead of auto playing the individual podcasts that I’ve added to my playlist, the app will either play ALL of the podcasts in numerical order (even ones I did not add to my playlist), or play random podcasts, including ones I’ve already listened to and are labeled “played”. What’s the point of allowing us to create playlists for podcasts if the app ignores them? Why label a podcast “played” if the app is going to just play it again anyways? Please fix

When I ask for George Strait songs and end up listening to some wrap music I have no interest in, I am not happy with your service at all.

Multiple long standing bugs. I love using this app, but there are many bugs that haven't been addressed in quite some time, with new ones stacking on top of them. The most recent one is when you filter to search for a song, when you queue it by swiping to the right, instead of queueing that song, it queues the overall first song in your library, which is very frustrating. Another is when you have the bar at the bottom and you try to swipe to the left to skip to the next song, it oftentimes glitches and either doesn't skip or sometimes even reverses to a previous song. Another is when you queue a song that you're listening to, it usually picks up from where you were in the song instead of the start. I'm sure there are others as well, and I feel like these are definitely solvable issues, and I don't know why they haven't been addressed yet. If it wasn't for the glaring bugs, this would be the best app on the app store by a long shot

I’m not sure if there was a glitch or what, but I’ve been charged for two accounts all this time. Customer service was nice, but I’m still out more than 150$ on a service I didn’t use. Let me see the songs. When you search up an artist it’s surprisingly difficult to just find their songs. Unless you search up a song in specific, listen to their albums/singles or check their discovery page to see their top 5 songs, (from my knowledge) you just don’t have access to a list of their songs. And I’m struggling to see why that makes sense. Wouldn’t it be kinda nice to be able to scroll through a list of your favorite artists songs, kinda like every other music app in existence, instead of having to tediously listen album by album or make the worlds longest queue. Understandably you can shuffle all of their music, pretty much making a list of all their songs pretty futile when you have all the albums and singles in order, but why can’t we just do both?

 All I ask: 

A list of just the songs

let me sort the list at the top
 (like newest-oldest or sum)

let me download this list. 

 If I’m interested in an artist then I want to hear more than just their top 5 songs, and I'm not listening to an album every time I want to find new music. The ideal fix to this problem would be to just take every song on each individual artist’s discovery page, then put them at the bottom of the discovery page. So that once you see all the albums and singles, then you just see all the songs. and you should be able to sort them, but throw some curveball with the sorting please, let’s do more than just newest-oldest and vise versa. Throw in like shortest-longest, fastest (tempo)-slowest, and more words - less words. Things that really might improve your music listening experience instead of just things for convenience

New ideas for enhance feature!. Let me start by saying I LOVE the enhance feature. It’s allowed me to find so many new songs that I adore and also create some banger playlists. As someone who listens to music religiously, and especially in the car, I’ve come across a problem. When I’m driving and I have my playlist enhanced, if a new song starts playing, it’s quite difficult to find that song in the playlist so that I can click the + button to add it. So I propose two new ideas! 

1) a “quick add” feature so that you can add the enhanced song to your playlist directly while you’re viewing the song in full screen, which would also add the 3 recommended songs

2) a “show this song in playlist” feature that automatically scrolls to the right spot in your playlist, allowing you to click the + button and then see the 3 recommended songs after

This would drastically increase the quality of life for me and others who listen to music all the time, but aren’t always able to spend a minute scrolling through my 20 hour long playlist trying to carefully spot the song that’s playing. Obviously I could just use the 3 dots and click “add to playlist” but then I don’t get the 3 recommended songs!!! All in all this is such a good feature and I love this app- these are just suggestions:)

Equalizer Improvements-Best music app around!. By far my favorite music streaming service. However the equalizer function is important to me as I frequently adjust it in order to get the best sound quality. The function is overall good but a way to save custom equalizer settings would make it much more user friendly. Additionally, placing it in a more accessible location would be extremely helpful. Currently it is buried in Settings->Playback->Equalizer forcing one to exit whatever they are doing to adjust sound settings. I hope to see either Playback Settings added to the Now Playing screen’s “three dot” menu or an additional “Equalizer” button created—opening Spotify’s equalizer over the current screen. This would allow the user to return to where they were immediately and greatly improve the app’s sound customization ability

Great with premium, wish I could say the same for without it.. This app isn’t really that bad when you have premium. It’s like a regular music app with some extra stuff. You can listen to music, watch episodes of podcasts and shows, and more. It’s pretty good.

Once you lose premium, however, it takes a nosedive in quality. You can only listen to playlists on shuffle and they add random songs to your playlists when you don’t want them to be there. Whenever you look up a specific song, it’ll be shuffled in a playlist with like multiple other songs, meaning you can’t listen to the song you want to listen to without having to listen to multiple others.

Sure, you can just skip the songs…but they put a limit on the skips. 6 per song. Wait an hour to get 6 more skips. And you may not be able to skip enough songs to reach the song you want to listen to. So it’s either wait, or skip and pray that you get the song you want to listen to.

It’s not a bad app with premium, but for the free version it’s just frustrating. Makes me think they intentionally designed it this way to get people to pay for premium, but what it ends up doing is just driving people away or having them switch to other platforms.

Impactful feature request: sort playlist by release date!. I see so many playlists that collect many tracks from across an artist’s entire discography, and it’s frustrating that tracks that are stylistically similar to each other or released around the same time aren’t always grouped together. The app currently allows sorting the tracks in a playlist by date added, album, etc. But there isn’t yet an option to sort chronologically by release date. “First released” and “Last Released” would be incredibly helpful as new sort options in playlists. That way, listeners can journey through an artist’s career in the order they actually released the music. It would make listeners happy, with the bonus of likely increasing total streaming time across your most engaged users who already spend a lot of time listening

In the future, all humor will be randomly generated.. This is a quote from VeggieTales. Now, although evidently meant to mock the modern absurdist take on humor, it raises an interesting question. What is humor? Where did it come from? What does it taste like? You see, humor, being a concept, is said to have no taste. Taste is the sensation felt in the mouth (or, if you are of the cephalopod persuasion, tentacles) when a substance comes in contact with it. The problem is that humor is a substance that cannot be easily applied to taste receptors. Therefore, the flavor of humor = N/A, or Not Applicable. Na is the chemical symbol for sodium, so we can safely conclude that humor tastes salty. Knowing that humor is salty explains how it can be that a non-random thing tends to be unfunny, a semi-random thing tends to be funny, and a completely random thing is unfunny. Previously, it had been concluded that if a quantity (A) had a quality (M), it must be true that A × 2 = B × 2. But because humor is salty, we know that it has the same unusual quality of saltiness where it is only good in moderation, disproving mathematics

Learning from Taylor's Practice. When I first listened to Taylor's podcast a year ago, I'll admit an unhealed judgey teenage part of myself listened with skepticism. I view Taylor as a teacher and the lessons hit me like art- sometimes what she shares resonates literally and other times the message flutters through my brain until it lands on the particular  petal of information it needs to connect with, and then other times I am happy for someone else to receive that message. My skeptical teenage brain is even welcomed by Taylor's own message which I deeply appreciate. It's such a whole space she creates. All are welcome in at whatever point they are at on their journey, and for me Taylor has been a trusted guide along my way! Thank you!

Great service, but new interface is garbage and search is worse. Another app that provides a fantastic service but thinks “innovation” is creating another echo chamber that feels like social media. And, there’s already a tab for it in my library, right there!

I go to “browse” for OTHER playlists and see what else is popular in the world, beyond my bubble. The “tailored” playlists aren’t “innovation”, it’s just another echo chamber. 

Also, y’all clearly take a ton of analytics on the music I listen to... but I can’t have a play count on my playlists, or see any of my usage like iTunes allows? I don’t need to be offered a “tailored” playlist that’s 95% songs I already have in a personal playlist, but it’d be nice for me to be able to actually reflect on what I listen to and how often - beyond the terrible “year in review” playlists that get pushed out every December

Annoying Bugs, So-so Navigation. Overall, this app is quite good. I like the flexibility and general ease-of-use. But there are some bugs that hamper the experience. The most annoying one is while using the search feature the back navigation buttons can disappear. It’s then impossible to go back and do another search. The app will eventually reset and make search available again but only if you force quit the app (which doesn’t always work) or wait a while until iOS restarts the app on its own. This is a major bug and I hope they fix soon. Another problem is that long titles get cut off in most screens and there’s really no way to see them. They definitely need to fix that. Also, I’m very glad they included an EQ. They didn’t have to do that; major props for doing so. But I do wish you could save custom settings and you didn’t have to dive so deep into the menus to get to it. Otherwise the app is OK but there is still much room for improvement

J’utilise Spotify depuis longtemps et c’est de loin la meilleure application de musique que j’ai essayée. L’interface est simple, moderne et très facile à utiliser. J’adore pouvoir découvrir de nouvelles chansons grâce aux playlists personnalisées et aux recommandations qui correspondent vraiment à mes goûts musicaux.

La qualité du son est excellente et il y a un immense choix de musique, de balados et de contenus pour tous les styles. J’aime aussi le fait de pouvoir créer mes propres playlists et les partager avec mes amis. L’application fonctionne très bien et je l’utilise tous les jours, que ce soit à la maison, à l’école, en voiture ou pendant mes activités.

Spotify rend l’écoute de musique agréable et pratique. Les mises à jour apportent régulièrement de nouvelles fonctionnalités intéressantes et l’expérience s’améliore constamment. Je recommande cette application à tous ceux qui aiment la musique. Merci à toute l’équipe Spotify pour cette excellente application !

Prices going up AGAIN. Long-time user, but I've reached a breaking point with Spotify's pricing direction in Canada.

As a student in Quebec, I'm ineligible for the student discount plan, which leaves me paying the full individual rate that is currently $14/month. Since 2023, the price has increased repeatedly with no meaningful improvement in the service to justify it. At that price point, Spotify is no longer competitive with alternatives that offer broader content (music, podcasts, and video) for the same or lower cost.

I'll be switching to YouTube Premium, which provides comparable audio streaming alongside additional features at a more justifiable rate for my situation. Unless Spotify revisits its regional pricing structure or expands student eligibility criteria in Quebec, I'll be cancelling my subscription.

It's a disappointing outcome after years of use, but the value proposition simply no longer holds

Easily the worst mobile music app. Spotify is good if you want to pay the overpriced monthly subscription. Otherwise, you can forget about choosing what songs to listen to and listening to your own playlists/albums without spotify deciding for you what you want to listen to. 

Without premium, you can’t just search for and play a song you want to listen to. It will force you to listen to random reccomended songs first, then a few bunches of 1:30 of unskippable ads, and then finally if you’re lucky it will play you the song you actually searched for. 
Without premium, if you make a playlist and want to listen to it, spotify forces its own “recommended similar songs” into your queue which are often more commonly played instead of the songs that are actually IN THE PLAYLIST YOU CREATED.
Too bad if you want to listen to an album, because spotify will instead choose other songs from other albums from the artist when you click play and then also sprinkle in some songs from entirely different artists and suddenly you’re an hour into listening and all you have heard is 20 minutes of ads, 2 album songs, 3 songs from different albums, and 6 songs from completely different artists.
Also, too bad if you want to skip the songs you didn’t ask for, because there is a limit on the amount of skips you can do per hour too.

Suggestions for classical music especially:. I listen to all sorts of music & this app is great for being able to find your way outside the usual “bubble”. But for people who listen to classical works there are a few issues that could probably be easily improved. 
First is to be able to see who has recorded a particular piece, then choose which soloist/conductor/orchestra/choir etc to download. At the moment there’s little choice, and it’s very difficult to see (at least on my iPhone 6s+ screen) who is featured. 
Second, when I am listening to something with a lot of movements I want to see quickly which one is playing- but the scrolling info is so slow that the screen goes back to sleep before the movement number goes across - or else it’s something like the Rachmaninov variations on a theme of Paganini, with 24 short sections and the movement is finished before its number reaches the screen. Can I set it to scroll faster? Or touch it to pause & enlarge to see all the info at once? 
Third thing is lack of important info like recording date & the other things mentioned above, ho was in he band for this recording etc 
Lastly atm, I haven’t figured out how to just play my playlist and then stop - it goes on to play random stuff and totally spoils the mood. Annoying. 
But overall a good app, worth the money

This is annoying at best, and problematic if the music is inappropriate in that setting (around children, in a workplace etc)

Poor setup with multiple problems. This app has a few major issues,
It didn’t recognise my wifi that had to be entered manually. 
The app gives you an error message in relation to the controller software.
It simply doesn’t work with either the speaker only set up or the bridge set up.
The only way I could get it working was to download controller software onto my windows laptop, set up wifi manually and the use the iphone app to join an existing system.
A novice wouldn’t have a hope. 
The app also doesn’t support the older Sonos br 100 zone bridge or zone player 5 series1. The colours referred to in their setup guide are different than on the equipment.
In summary this is a very poor app if you have older equipment. I have no idea if it’s any good on the newer equipment, you would hope so. 
On a positive note, now that I have it set up, it is working fine and I’m enjoying using it.
Hopefully this review will help others get theirs going if they have the older Sonos equipment

Version 8.9.44: great music app + how to make it better. NOTE: this review is about the premium version.

PROS:
Instant access to more music than you could listen to in a lifetime.

It can be connected to the SoundHound app, so that any songs identified by SoundHound are automatically added to a SoundHound playlist.

Playlists based on your history help you discover new music that you’re likely to enjoy.

Regular updates to squash bugs and improve the experience.

HOW TO MAKE IT BETTER:
When changing the settings for a song (e.g. repeat), the button at the bottom of the screen should say "Done" instead of "Cancel" - you’re closing the settings, not canceling them.

Make the “don't like” button available on every song, just like the “like” button.

Make the sorting options easier to see, instead of hiding them so that they’re only visible when we pull the screen down from the top.

CONCLUSION: A great app, with a little room for improvement - 5 stars
